title: [podman/4.6.1, podman/4.9.4] Intermittent failure in playing kube file
body:
### Issue Description

I am runnnig rootless podman with the following way of starting/stopping podman.
`systemd` is used to launch bash script which call `podman` CLI to play kube file.
The pod/container was not able to run reliably after upgrading from podman 4.4.1 to 4.6.1/4.9.4. I didn't test with 4.5.X to 4.8.X

When It failed, there is the following message:
```
systemd[1]: Cannot find unit for notify message of PID 709497, ignoring.
```
The systemd files below are just one of the systemd unit file. There are multiple of them and also not able to start reliably. 


### Steps to reproduce the issue

## Systemd unit file: webapi_pod.service
```
[Unit]
Description=WebAPI Pod Service
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target

[Service]
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStartSec=120
TimeoutStopSec=180
RestartSec=30

User=rootless_podman
Group=rootless_podman

ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/start_pod_slirp.sh \
                yaml_folder_root \
                webapi_pod.yaml \
                webapi_configmap.yaml \
                container_config_folder_root \
                log_folder_root
ExecStop=/usr/local/bin/stop_pod.sh \
                yaml_folder_root  \
                pod.yaml
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

## start_pod_slirp.sh
```bash
#!/bin/bash

export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
USER_ID=$(id -u)
export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR='/run/user/'${USER_ID}
export DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS='unix:path=/run/user/'${USER_ID} 

YAML_DIR=$1
KUBE_YAML=$2
TEMP_KUBE_YAML=_TEMP_$2
CONFIGMAP_YAML=$3
export PERSISTENCE_PATH=$4
export LOG_PERSISTENCE_PATH=$5
dos2unix $YAML_DIR/$KUBE_YAML
dos2unix $YAML_DIR/$CONFIGMAP_YAML
envsubst '$PERSISTENCE_PATH $LOG_PERSISTENCE_PATH' < $YAML_DIR/$KUBE_YAML > $YAML_DIR/$TEMP_KUBE_YAML

cd /tmp
podman  --log-level=debug  play kube $YAML_DIR/$TEMP_KUBE_YAML  --replace --service-container=true --configmap=$YAML_DIR/$CONFIGMAP_YAML --network=slirp4netns:port_handler=slirp4netns 
```

## webapi_pod.yaml
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  annotations:
    io.kubernetes.cri-o.ContainerType/web: container
    io.kubernetes.cri-o.SandboxID/web: 2e230e461afd9f80a18711c24c32d10f7cf0e390e7c0eebc1ab9fcacd141921
    io.kubernetes.cri-o.TTY/web: "true"
    io.podman.annotations.autoremove/web: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.init/web: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.privileged/web: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.publish-all/web: "FALSE"
  creationTimestamp: "2022-11-26T10:03:09Z"
  labels:
    app: webapi_pod
  name: webapi_pod
spec:
  selector:
    app: webapi_pod
  type: NodePort
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  annotations:
    io.kubernetes.cri-o.ContainerType/web: container
    io.kubernetes.cri-o.SandboxID/web: 2e230e461afd9f80a18711c24c32d10f7cf0e390e7c0eebc1ab9fcacd141921
    io.kubernetes.cri-o.TTY/web: "true"
    io.podman.annotations.autoremove/web: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.init/web: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.privileged/web: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.publish-all/web: "FALSE"
  creationTimestamp: "2022-11-26T10:03:09Z"
  labels:
    app: webapi_pod
  name: webapi_pod
spec:
  hostname: webapi_pod
  restartPolicy: Always
  containers:
  - name: webapi
    image: localhost/example/rmf/webapi-app:latest
    ports:
    - name: http
      hostPort: 18010 
      containerPort: 80
    envFrom:
    - configMapRef:
        name: webapi-env-configMap
    volumeMounts:
      - mountPath: "/var/log/webapi"
        name: webapi-log
      - mountPath: "/uploadfiles"
        name: webapi-uploadfiles
      - mountPath: "/certs"
        name: webapi-certs
    resources: {}
    securityContext:
      seLinuxOptions: 
        type:  spc_t
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - CAP_MKNOD
    tty: true
  volumes:
  - name: webapi-log
    hostPath:
      path: ${LOG_PERSISTENCE_PATH}/webapi
      type: Directory
  - name: webapi-uploadfiles
    hostPath:
      path: ${PERSISTENCE_PATH}/webapi/uploadfiles
      type: Directory
  - name: webapi-certs
    hostPath:
      path: ${PERSISTENCE_PATH}/certs
      type: Directory 
  
status: {}
```




### Describe the results you received

The pod are not able to start reliably. 
Refer to [this ](https://gist.github.com/cwrx777/7fbd26123cf77bc9c7dbff35cd1677a0)for podman log when it failed to start.
Refer to [this ](https://gist.github.com/cwrx777/91ca40a9a6015eede425893f6426ca65)for podman log when it started successfully.

### Describe the results you expected

the pod can start/stop reliably.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
We do support running with User= in a systemd unit (https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/20573)

Also we recommend using quadlet now to run kube files under systemd, we cannot possible support all the custom user created units

Hi,

do you have any clue on why it's intermittently behaving this way after 4.4.1? is it because of quadlet ?

It is not related to quadlet as this just generates unit files.

If you have a specific error that really only happens after the podman update, you can compile podman from source and git bisect it to find the root commit then we might have some idea what went wrong.
